title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Production Prompt Mission
summary: A software maintenance team. The team needs one task contract adapted and verified for both Claude Code and Codex. Planned artifact: Deliver Cross-Agent Repository Change Brief with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-review-frame-8qk6eu" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Review Frame into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-review-frame-8qk6eu
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Review Frame condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Review Frame applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define review baseline, risk categories, and evidence required for actionable findings.
